title: GitLab Deletes Production Database (2017)
summary: An engineer accidentally ran rm -rf on the production database directory during routine maintenance. 300GB of data lost, with only a 6-hour-old backup available.
lesson: Always double-check which terminal you're typing in.

title: Amazon S3 Outage (2017)
summary: A typo in a command during routine debugging took down a massive portion of S3 in us-east-1, cascading across the internet. Countless services went offline.
lesson: One region to rule them all is not a resilience strategy.

title: Knight Capital Trading Glitch (2012)
summary: A deployment error activated old test code on Knight Capital's trading servers, causing the firm to lose $440 million in 45 minutes.
lesson: Clean up dead code. It might not be as dead as you think.

title: Facebook Global Outage (2021)
summary: A routine BGP maintenance command withdrew Facebook's DNS routes, taking down Facebook, Instagram, and WhatsApp for over 6 hours worldwide.
lesson: Don't lock yourself out of the building while doing maintenance on the locks.

title: Cloudflare Regex Outage (2019)
summary: A single regex rule deployed to all Cloudflare edge servers caused CPU spikes to 100%, taking down millions of websites for 27 minutes.
lesson: Regex is a superpower. And like all superpowers, it can destroy the world.

title: CrowdStrike Blue Screen Apocalypse (2024)
summary: A faulty content update to CrowdStrike Falcon caused millions of Windows machines worldwide to enter a boot loop, grounding flights and halting hospitals.
lesson: Kernel-level code deserves kernel-level testing.

title: GitHub Universe of 500 Errors (2018)
summary: A network partition during a database migration caused GitHub to be down or degraded for over 24 hours, affecting millions of developers.
lesson: Database migrations and network partitions: a love story that always ends badly.

title: NPM Left-Pad Incident (2016)
summary: A developer unpublished an 11-line npm package called left-pad, breaking thousands of builds including React, Babel, and more.
lesson: Your 11-line utility might be holding the internet together.

title: Roblox 73-Hour Outage (2021)
summary: A subtle bug in Roblox's internal service discovery (related to HashiCorp Consul) caused a cascading failure that took the platform offline for 73 hours.
lesson: Service meshes can turn a small fire into a wildfire.

title: Fastly CDN Global Outage (2021)
summary: A single customer configuration change triggered a latent bug in Fastly's software, knocking major websites offline for about an hour.
lesson: One customer's config change can be the internet's config change.

title: Google Cloud Networking Outage (2019)
summary: A configuration change intended for a small number of servers was applied globally, causing severe network congestion across multiple Google Cloud regions.
lesson: Blast radius should be measured before pressing enter.

title: British Airways IT Meltdown (2017)
summary: A power supply issue at a data center was compounded by an uncontrolled restart, grounding all flights for days and costing over £100M.
lesson: Your disaster recovery plan should survive its first contact with an actual disaster.

title: Slack's Database Migration Surprise (2022)
summary: A routine database migration at Slack caused unexpected locking, leading to degraded service during peak work hours.
lesson: Migrate databases like you defuse bombs: carefully and during off-hours.

title: Azure DNS Global Outage (2021)
summary: An update to Azure DNS triggered a cascading failure affecting Azure services globally, including Teams, Xbox Live, and the Azure Portal itself.
lesson: DNS is always the answer. And sometimes the problem.

title: Heroku Security Incident (2022)
summary: Attackers accessed Heroku's GitHub integration OAuth tokens, forcing a revocation that broke thousands of CI/CD pipelines overnight.
lesson: Supply chain security: because your deploy depends on everyone else's security too.

title: Travis CI Secret Exposure (2021)
summary: A vulnerability in Travis CI exposed secrets from public repositories, including signing keys and access tokens from major open-source projects.
lesson: Your CI/CD pipeline is a vault. Treat it like one.

title: Tesla Firmware Bricking (2020)
summary: A firmware update caused a fleet of Tesla vehicles to display errors and lose functionality, requiring service center visits to fix.
lesson: OTA updates for cars: because bricking phones wasn't exciting enough.

title: Spotify's Leap Second Crash (2012)
summary: The Linux kernel's handling of a leap second caused high CPU usage across Spotify's servers, leading to a multi-hour outage.
lesson: Time is an illusion. Leap seconds are a hostile illusion.

title: AWS DynamoDB Outage (2015)
summary: A metadata partition in DynamoDB became overloaded, causing cascading failures across us-east-1 that affected Netflix, Reddit, and others.
lesson: Metadata about your data can be more dangerous than the data itself.

title: CircleCI Security Breach (2023)
summary: CircleCI urged all customers to rotate their secrets after discovering unauthorized access to customer data, disrupting CI/CD pipelines worldwide.
lesson: Rotate your secrets regularly, or someone else will rotate your weekend plans.

title: The Y2K Deploy That Actually Broke (2000)
summary: A financial institution's Y2K remediation patch introduced a new bug that miscalculated interest for the first week of January 2000.
lesson: The fix for the bug is often the source of the next bug.

title: Etsy's 2-Hour Homepage Blank (2014)
summary: A deploy removed a critical template file, turning Etsy's homepage into a blank white page for two hours during peak shopping.
lesson: Smoke tests exist for a reason. Use them.

title: Twitter's Fail Whale Era (2008-2012)
summary: Twitter's rapid growth outpaced its Ruby on Rails infrastructure, causing frequent outages memorialized by the famous Fail Whale error page.
lesson: Scaling is not a tomorrow problem. It's a yesterday problem you ignored.

title: Valve Steam Store Caching Disaster (2015)
summary: A caching misconfiguration on Christmas Day caused Steam users to see other users' account details, including purchase history and partial payment info.
lesson: Cache invalidation and deploying on Christmas: two of the hardest problems.

title: Atlassian Cloud Deletion (2022)
summary: An internal maintenance script accidentally deleted data for approximately 400 customers, taking up to two weeks to fully restore.
lesson: Deletion scripts should have more safety checks than a nuclear launch.

title: The MongoDB Default Config Apocalypse (2017)
summary: Thousands of MongoDB instances were wiped by attackers exploiting the default configuration that required no authentication.
lesson: Defaults matter. Especially the ones that leave the door unlocked.

title: Dark Souls Servers Permanently Offline (2022)
summary: A remote code execution vulnerability in Dark Souls multiplayer servers forced FromSoftware to take PC servers offline indefinitely.
lesson: Player-to-player connections can also be attacker-to-server connections.

title: OVHcloud Data Center Fire (2021)
summary: A fire at OVHcloud's Strasbourg data center destroyed servers hosting millions of websites, with some customers losing all their data permanently.
lesson: Cloud doesn't mean fireproof. Have off-site backups. Real off-site.

title: Zoom's Monday Morning Outage (2020)
summary: Zoom went down on the first Monday of the school year as millions tried to log in simultaneously for remote learning.
lesson: Load testing should simulate your worst Monday, not your best Tuesday.

title: Let's Encrypt Certificate Revocation (2020)
summary: Let's Encrypt had to revoke over 3 million certificates due to a bug in their CAA checking code, causing mass HTTPS disruption.
lesson: Certificate authorities: because breaking HTTPS at scale requires trust.

title: Samsung Galaxy Note 7 OTA Recall (2016)
summary: Samsung pushed firmware updates trying to fix battery issues that were causing phones to catch fire, eventually recalling the entire product line.
lesson: Some bugs can't be fixed with a software update.

title: The Log4Shell Zero-Day (2021)
summary: A critical RCE vulnerability in Log4j (CVE-2021-44228) affected virtually every Java application, causing a worldwide scramble to patch.
lesson: Your logging library should log events, not execute arbitrary code.

title: Snapchat's Redesign Deployment (2018)
summary: Snapchat rolled out a widely hated redesign that caused a petition with 1.2 million signatures and a permanent loss of daily active users.
lesson: Sometimes the riskiest deploy is the one that works exactly as planned.

title: Apple Maps Launch (2012)
summary: Apple's replacement of Google Maps with its own Maps app was so buggy that Tim Cook publicly apologized and recommended competitors.
lesson: Shipping on time means nothing if it ships you off a bridge.

title: Healthcare.gov Launch Disaster (2013)
summary: The U.S. healthcare marketplace website crashed on launch day and remained barely functional for months, handling only a fraction of expected traffic.
lesson: Load test with the actual expected load, not 1% of it.

title: Twitter's Elon Era Migration Outage (2023)
summary: Aggressive infrastructure changes at Twitter caused intermittent outages and rate limiting that affected millions of users.
lesson: Moving fast and breaking things is a strategy. It's just not a good one.

title: Dropbox Accidentally Unlocks All Accounts (2011)
summary: A code update removed authentication checks for 4 hours, allowing anyone to access any Dropbox account with any password.
lesson: Auth bypass bugs: because who needs passwords anyway?

title: MySpace Data Loss (2019)
summary: MySpace lost 12 years of user-uploaded music (approximately 50 million songs) during a server migration gone wrong.
lesson: If the migration plan doesn't include backups, it's not a plan. It's a prayer.

title: Datadog's Agent Update Crash (2023)
summary: A Datadog agent update caused high CPU usage across customer infrastructure, ironically degrading the systems it was supposed to monitor.
lesson: The monitoring tool should not become the thing being monitored.

title: Sonos Bricking Update (2020)
summary: A Sonos app update rendered older speakers unusable, forcing customers to either upgrade or lose multi-room audio functionality.
lesson: Planned obsolescence via software update is still planned obsolescence.

title: Toyota Unintended Acceleration (2009-2011)
summary: Software defects in Toyota's engine control systems contributed to unintended acceleration incidents, leading to massive recalls and congressional hearings.
lesson: When your software controls a 2-ton vehicle, testing is not optional.

title: Reddit's Great Configuration Migration (2023)
summary: A Kubernetes migration at Reddit caused intermittent outages during peak hours as services were moved between clusters.
lesson: Kubernetes migrations: because regular migrations weren't stressful enough.

title: Notion's 3-Hour Outage (2023)
summary: A database issue at Notion caused the app to be inaccessible for 3 hours, leaving teams unable to access their documentation.
lesson: When your docs platform goes down, nobody knows the runbook to bring it back up.

title: WhatsApp's New Year Crash (2018)
summary: WhatsApp servers crashed under the load of New Year's messages, leaving billions unable to send midnight greetings.
lesson: If you know the traffic spike is coming, prepare for it. It comes every year.

title: Linus Torvalds Breaks Git Merge (2022)
summary: A kernel merge window resulted in a broken build that took a day to diagnose, proving even Linus can have a bad merge day.
lesson: If Linus can break a build, so can you. Don't feel bad. Feel prepared.

title: AWS ap-southeast-2 Power Event (2023)
summary: A power event in AWS Sydney region caused multi-hour outages for EC2, RDS, and related services affecting Australian businesses.
lesson: Multi-AZ doesn't help when the power company has other plans.

title: The DNS Change That Split the Internet (2016)
summary: A DDoS attack on DNS provider Dyn took down Twitter, Netflix, Reddit, and others by targeting a single point of failure in DNS resolution.
lesson: DNS is always the single point of failure you forgot about.

title: Windows 10 Update Deletes User Files (2018)
summary: The Windows 10 October 2018 Update deleted users' Documents folder contents, forcing Microsoft to pull the update entirely.
lesson: Never delete user data. Especially when they didn't ask you to.

title: Pokemon GO Launch Day (2016)
summary: Pokemon GO's server infrastructure was overwhelmed on launch day, with load 50x the expected maximum, causing days of intermittent outages.
lesson: When your game makes people walk outside, your servers need to keep up.

title: Robinhood Leap Day Outage (2020)
summary: Robinhood's trading platform went down on a Monday after leap day due to a date-handling bug, during a historic market rally.
lesson: Leap years happen every 4 years. Test for them every year.
